canonical_definition: Across the provided sources, CUB is consistently identified as the Caltech-UCSD Birds dataset, often with the full name Caltech-UCSD Birds-200-2011. The prevailing usage of this instrument is as a benchmark for fine-grained classification tasks. It is most commonly defined as a dataset for "fine-grained image classification" that specifically features 200 species of birds. A similar framing describes it as a "fine-grained visual classification dataset." One paper also notes its application in "training and evaluating localization capabilities." The dataset is situated alongside other image classification benchmarks, as one source lists it with CIFAR-10, Food-101, and Flower.
papers: Safety Alignment via Constrained Knowledge Unlearning
How Sememic Components Can Benefit Link Prediction for Lexico-Semantic Knowledge Graphs?
UniversalCEFR: Enabling Open Multilingual Research on Language Proficiency Assessment

definitions.text: The Caltech-UCSD Birds-200-2011 dataset, a benchmark for fine-grained image classification featuring 200 species of birds.
